Powell's last meeting: What Fed rhetoric may look like under Kevin WarshWall Street expects the Federal Reserve to hold interest rates steady at what is possibly Jerome Powell's final FOMC meeting as Fed chair on Wednesday. Many investors are speculating whether Powell will stay on the Fed's Board of Governors after his term at the US central bank concludes on May 15. Thornburg Investment Management head of fixed income Christian Hoffmann and 42 Macro founder and CEO Darius Dale come on Market Domination to talk about what investors are expecting from Powell's press conference tomorrow, and what may change for the Fed if Kevin Warsh is confirmed to succeed Powell.
Trump is still holding 'a sword' above Fed's Powell: Sen. Warren
Big Tech: What this strategist is looking for on earnings calls

Oil prices rise despite UAE exit from OPEC as Iran war ceasefire hangs in balanceOil markets are navigating a period of unprecedented volatility after the UAE announced its withdrawal from OPEC and the OPEC+.View on euronews

Fed meeting today: Central bank 'firmly parked' on interest rate pauseThe Federal Reserve is widely expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady Wednesday afternoon amid uncertainty over when the conflict in the Middle East will be resolved.
German, Spanish Inflation Reach Multiyear HighsConsumer prices were 2.9% higher in April than a year earlier in Germany, an increase in the annual rate of inflation from 2.8% in March.
Oil prices top $115 as Iran war talks stall over HormuzU.S. gas prices are hovering near highs not seen since the start of the Iran war as Washington weighs extending its blockade of Iranian ports

Elizabeth Warren: Trump is wielding a sword over the FedPresident Trump is using the threat of criminal prosecution as a sword he's holding over the Federal Reserve, Democratic Sen. Elizabeth Warren said in an interview with Yahoo Finance on Tuesday.

Oil prices jump, erasing ceasefire losses, on reports President Trump will reject Iran's peace planOil prices rose on Tuesday as the White House is reportedly set to deny a peace plan from Iran, while the UAE said it would leave OPEC in May.
Gold, silver plunge amid US-Iran stalemateGold and silver moved lower as oil topped $110 per barrel, raising concerns that central banks will have to hold rates higher for longer.
Travelers are starting to balk at sky-high airline ticket prices, BofA data showsConsumers are starting to push back against the airlines.
Consumer confidence ticks higher as better vibes on job market take holdConsumer confidence in April reached its highest level so far this year, according to a survey from the Conference Board.
Powell's term as Fed chair is ending. Here's why he could stick around longer.Jerome Powell's term as Fed chair concludes May 15, but he could continue as a governor until 2028.
